description: Offline speech-to-text for voice notes. A one-shot whisper.cpp engine that bootstraps its own ffmpeg + whisper-cli + model on first use; audio never leaves your machine.
configHelp: Transcriber turns voice notes into text, fully offline. It runs whisper.cpp's `whisper-cli` once per clip: ffmpeg decodes the audio to a 16 kHz WAV, whisper-cli transcribes it, and the text is inserted for you. On first use it installs everything it needs automatically — on macOS via `brew install whisper-cpp ffmpeg`, on Windows/Linux by downloading prebuilt whisper.cpp and ffmpeg binaries plus the model into ~/.codeterm/transcriber. No server, no setup. Two knobs (codeterm plugin config transcriber --set key=value): `model` — the whisper model to use, default `small` (multilingual, ~466 MB, good accuracy/speed; try `base` for faster/lighter or `medium`/`large-v3` for higher accuracy); `language` — a BCP-47 hint like `en` or `ru`, default `auto` to detect per clip. Ask the user which language they mostly speak and whether they want a faster or more accurate model, then set those two keys.
